body.title: Device registry: add agent_class field to manifest
body.result: Added AGENT_CLASS to BaseDevice + named devices; registry stores agent_class; datacenter_manifest exposes devices section with agent_class per device; librarian self-declares in manifest_tools.py; 12 new tests green
body.description:
Add `agent_class` as a declared field on device registrations so `datacenter_manifest` returns the class per device. Devices self-declare their class in their `who_am_i()` return dict.

Field values: `'utility'` | `'specialized'` | `'general'`. Default: `'utility'` (safest assumption for new devices). Existing devices get explicit declarations: igor→general, granny→specialized, nanny→specialized, minion→utility, scraps→utility, librarian→utility, google_secretary→utility.

**Affected files:** skeleton/registry.py (add agent_class to device record schema); unseen_university/shim.py or BaseDevice.who_am_i() (add agent_class field with default); config/profiles/*.yaml (add agent_class per device); tests/test_registry_agent_class.py (new)

**Design rules:** OOP-first — who_am_i() is the declaration point; skeleton/registry.py owns the aggregation; no standalone functions

**Scope boundary:** IN — field declaration + manifest exposure + existing device tagging. OUT — Granny routing changes based on class (follow-on); capability filtering by class.

**Completion criteria:** (1) `datacenter_manifest` JSON includes `agent_class` for every registered device; (2) `who_am_i()` in BaseDevice has agent_class with default `'utility'`; (3) igor/granny/nanny/minion/scraps/librarian all have explicit class values; (4) tests/test_registry_agent_class.py green
